src_embedding_text: source: design test case id: tc-authentication-001 title: successful user login with valid credentials module: authentication source files or pages: 3, 6 covered methods: preconditions: - user is registered in the system. - database contains the user’s hashed password. test data: - email: `volunteer1@example.com` - password: `securepass123!` test steps: 1. user enters email and password in the login form. 2. system sends `post /login` request to the server. 3. server queries the database for the user with `email = "volunteer1@example.com"`. 4. server verifies the password using `bcrypt.comparesync()`. 5. server generates a jwt token. 6. server updates the `tokens` table to revoke old tokens and inserts a new one. 7. server sends a success response with the jwt token to the client. expected result: - user is redirected to the home page. - jwt token is stored in the client (e.g., localstorage). - no error messages are displayed. unit: project:
